An undated photo shows the historic fortified city of Carcassonne, France. /CFP

As the Olympic torch blazes its path through history, it illuminates the rich cultural heritage of two iconic cities: Carcassonne in France and Pingyao in China.

In southern France, Carcassonneâs ancient walls whisper tales of a storied past. This historic fortified city, with its 2,000-year-old medieval castle, is a UNESCO World Heritage Site that transports visitors to a bygone era. Alongside the majestic castle, the Canal du Midi, another UNESCO World Heritage Site, connects the Atlantic Ocean with the Mediterranean Sea, adding to the cityâs allure.

An undated photo shows part of the ancient city wall of Pingyao in Shanxi Province, China. /CFP

In Shanxi Province, China, the ancient city of Pingyao stands as a cultural heritage site with a history spanning over 2,800 years. Renowned for its well-preserved ancient layout and architectural style, Pingyao offers a glimpse into Chinaâs rich past. As visitors stroll through the gray brick alleys, explore the old city walls, temples, and traditional banks, they are transported back in time, experiencing the profound historical ambiance of this UNESCO World Heritage Site.

Together, Carcassonne and Pingyao highlight the historical and cultural grandeur of both France and China. These cities, celebrated in the journeys of the Olympic torch, underscore the shared human spirit that transcends time and geography.
